creator: denverh@comcast.net
text:
Interesting.  Those are the same versions I have on 14.1 where it doesn't work.  And apparently at least one other person has run into the same issue.  I would be willing to suspect that it's something with my specific system, except that it will do exactly the same thing with a fresh install of 14.1 on a different system with not much more than xorg and zim installed, a freshly created new user, and the default twm window manager.

I understand that if you can't duplicate this then it's going to be difficult to do much more, although I'm willing to try any suggestions.
